original_hypothesis.title: All-Owner Removal Creates Permanent Irrecoverable Fund Lock
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: MultiOwnable.removeOwnerAtIndex has no minimum owner count check. An owner can systematically remove all owners including themselves, leaving zero owners. With zero owners, no entity can pass the _checkOwner() check, making addOwnerAddress, addOwnerPublicKey, execute, executeBatch, and _authorizeUpgrade all permanently inaccessible. All funds (ETH, tokens, NFTs) in the wallet become permanently locked with no recovery mechanism. The EntryPoint path also fails since validateUserOp requires a valid signature from a registered owner.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: removeOwnerAtIndex only checks that the owner exists at the given index, then deletes it. There is no check ensuring at least one owner remains. This is a code defect: the function should enforce count(activeOwners) >= 1.

proof_of_signal.pattern: removeOwnerAtIndex deletes owner without checking remaining owner count; no minimum owner enforcement anywhere

preconditions: Attacker must be a current wallet owner (compromised key or malicious insider)
Owner calls removeOwnerAtIndex for every active owner index

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: After removing all owners: isOwnerAddress(anyone) returns false, _checkOwner always reverts
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Compromised owner or social engineering to remove all owners as grief attack
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit to attacker (grief attack) — victim loses all wallet funds permanently

economic_questions: Is there any recovery mechanism once all owners are removed?
Can the EntryPoint path work without any registered owners?
Can a UUPS upgrade restore owner access?
validation_requirements: Remove all owners in test, confirm all owner-gated functions revert
Confirm no path exists to re-add owners (initialize reverts if nextOwnerIndex > 0)

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: N/A — victim loss is permanent lock of all funds

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% permanent loss of all wallet assets

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm no recovery path exists after zero-owner state

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
